drugs.notes: 150mg twice a day (BD), days 1-28 inclusive all cycles. Swallowed whole, with or without food. If dose missed within 6 hours take as soon as possible; if >6 hours skip missed dose.
Cycle 1 Day 1 only: 100mg flat dose in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 120 minutes.
Cycle 1 Day 2 only: 325mg/m² in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% starting at 50mg/hour, increasing by 50mg/hour every 30 minutes to max 400mg/hour. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg.

Cycle 1 Day 14: 500mg/m² in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% as per local rituximab administration guidelines. Cycle 2 Days 1 and 14: 500mg/m². Cycles 3-6 Day 1: 500mg/m². If lymphocyte >25x10^9/L on day 1 consider fractionating: Day 1 125mg/m² in 100ml, Day 2 375mg/m² in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9%. [VERIFIED: Rituximab 500mg/m² from cycle 2 onwards in CLL is standard per MHRA SmPC and NICE TA174 — higher than lymphoma dosing of 375mg/m²]

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils 0.5-1.0 x10^9/L
dose_modifications.notes: Maintain idelalisib dose and monitor neutrophil count weekly.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils <0.5 x10^9/L
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt idelalisib and monitor counts weekly. Once counts recover, restart at 100mg twice a day.

dose_modifications.condition: ALT 3-5x ULN
dose_modifications.notes: Monitor liver function more regularly. Continue idelalisib with closer monitoring.

dose_modifications.condition: ALT >5x ULN
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue idelalisib until ALT <3x ULN. Restart at 100mg twice a day, may increase to 150mg twice a day if liver function remains stable. If ALT rises again >5x ULN, withhold until <3x ULN and consider restarting at 100mg twice a day at consultant discretion.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC Grade 3 or above non-haematological toxicity (first occurrence)
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until AE resolves to grade 2 or below, then reduce to 75% of original dose.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC Grade 3 or above non-haematological toxicity (recurrence)
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until recovery then reduce to 50% of original dose or discontinue as appropriate.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC Grade 3 or above diarrhoea
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold idelalisib. Restart once resolved to grade 1 or less at 100mg twice a day. If no recurrence, may increment to 150mg twice a day.

dose_modifications.condition: Suspected pneumonitis
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold idelalisib and treat accordingly. Once resolved and re-treatment appropriate, resume at 100mg twice a day.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC Grade 3 or above rash
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold idelalisib. Once resolved to grade 1 or less, restart at 100mg twice a day. Consider re-escalation to 150mg twice a day if rash does not recur.

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg intravenous (30 minutes prior to rituximab)
Hydrocortisone 100mg intravenous (30 minutes prior to rituximab)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (30 minutes prior to rituximab)
Aciclovir 400mg twice a day oral (anti-infective prophylaxis)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg once a day Monday, Wednesday and Friday oral (for up to 6 months post-treatment)
Allopurinol 300mg once a day oral for 7 days (cycle 1 only, TLS prophylaxis)
special_warnings: CMV PCR in blood (EDTA) should be monitored every 4 weeks; idelalisib must be discontinued during confirmed CMV viraemia (PCR positivity in more than two consecutive samples taken one week apart).
Hepatitis B status must be checked prior to starting rituximab.
Risk of tumour lysis syndrome (TLS); allopurinol prophylaxis 300mg once daily for 7 days in first cycle; IV rasburicase may be considered in high-risk individuals.

Progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML) risk with rituximab; monitor for neurological/cognitive/psychiatric symptoms; suspend dosing if PML suspected and permanently discontinue if confirmed.
Idelalisib associated with many drug interactions; check all concomitant medications.
Idelalisib associated with hepatotoxicity, colitis, pneumonitis, CMV reactivation; regular monitoring required.
If lymphocyte count >25x10^9/L on day 1, consider fractionating rituximab dose.
National Patient Safety Alert (NPSA/2008/RRR001) applies to idelalisib as oral chemotherapy.

drugs.notes: 600mg once daily (days 1-28 inclusive). Take with or just after food with a full glass of water. Starting dose may be increased to 800mg (given as 400mg twice daily) in accelerated phase or blast crisis per European Haematology Association Guidelines. Available as 400mg or 100mg tablets.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils <0.5 x10^9/L AND/OR platelets <10 x10^9/L (accelerated phase/blast crisis) - cytopenia unrelated to leukaemia
dose_modifications.notes: Check marrow aspirate/biopsy. If cytopenia unrelated to leukaemia, reduce to 400mg. If persists 2 weeks reduce to 300mg. If persists 4 weeks and still unrelated to leukaemia, stop until neutrophils ≥1 and platelets ≥20x10^9/L, then resume at 300mg once a day.

dose_modifications.condition: Bilirubin >3x ULN or AST/ALT >5x ULN - hepatic impairment
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until bilirubin <1.5x ULN or ALT/AST <2.5x ULN. Resume at reduced dose: 600mg to 400mg, 800mg to 600mg.

special_warnings: Philadelphia chromosome-positive CML must be confirmed before treatment.
Imatinib is a CYP3A4 substrate; avoid strong CYP3A4 inhibitors/inducers; check all drug interactions.
Check Hepatitis B, C and HIV status prior to starting therapy.
Consider baseline LVEF assessment in patients with known cardiac disease or elderly patients.
Long-term imatinib may be associated with clinically significant renal function decline; monitor closely.
National Patient Safety Alert (NPSA/2008/RRR001) applies to imatinib as oral chemotherapy.
Doses of 800mg must be given as 400mg twice daily.
12 cycles will be set in ARIA; treatment continues until disease progression or intolerance.

drugs.notes: 400mg once daily (days 1-28 inclusive). Take with or just after food with a full glass of water. Starting dose may be increased to 600mg or 800mg (400mg twice daily) in chronic phase per European Haematology Association Guidelines. Available as 400mg or 100mg tablets.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils <1 x10^9/L AND/OR platelets <50 x10^9/L (chronic phase CML) - 1st occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Stop treatment until neutrophils ≥1.5x10^9/L and platelets ≥75x10^9/L. 1st occurrence: continue at 100% dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils <1 x10^9/L AND/OR platelets <50 x10^9/L (chronic phase CML) - 2nd occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Stop treatment until neutrophils ≥1.5x10^9/L and platelets ≥75x10^9/L. 2nd occurrence: reduce to 300mg.

dose_modifications.condition: Bilirubin >3x ULN or AST/ALT >5x ULN - hepatic impairment
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until bilirubin <1.5x ULN or ALT/AST <2.5x ULN. Resume at reduced dose: 400mg to 300mg, 600mg to 400mg, 800mg to 600mg.

special_warnings: Philadelphia chromosome-positive CML must be confirmed before treatment.
Imatinib is a CYP3A4 substrate; avoid strong CYP3A4 inhibitors/inducers; check all drug interactions.
Check Hepatitis B, C and HIV status prior to starting therapy.
Consider baseline LVEF assessment in patients with known cardiac disease or elderly patients.
Long-term imatinib may be associated with clinically significant renal function decline; monitor closely.
National Patient Safety Alert (NPSA/2008/RRR001) applies to imatinib as oral chemotherapy.
Doses of 800mg must be given as 400mg twice daily.
12 cycles will be set in ARIA; treatment continues until disease progression or intolerance.

drugs.notes: Cycle 1 Day 1: 0.8mg/m² IV infusion over 60 minutes in 50mL sodium chloride 0.9%. Cycle 1 is a 21-day cycle (can be extended to 28 days if CR/CRi achieved or to allow toxicity recovery). Cycles 2-6 are 28-day cycles. If CR/CRi achieved prescribe 0.5mg/m² for all doses in cycles 2-6. If CR/CRi not achieved, cycles 2-6 Day 1 dose remains 0.8mg/m². Protect from light during administration.

Cycle 1 Days 8 and 15: 0.5mg/m² IV infusion over 60 minutes in 50mL sodium chloride 0.9%. Cycles 2-6 Days 8 and 15: 0.5mg/m² (for all patients regardless of response). Protect from light during administration. Minimum 6 days between doses within a cycle.

dose_modifications.condition: ANC was ≥1x10^9/L pre-treatment and decreases during treatment
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt next cycle until ANC recovers to ≥1x10^9/L.

dose_modifications.condition: Platelet count was ≥50x10^9/L pre-treatment and decreases during treatment
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt next cycle until platelet count recovers to ≥50x10^9/L.

dose_modifications.condition: ANC and/or platelet count was <1x10^9/L and/or <50x10^9/L pre-treatment and decreases further
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt next cycle until ANC and platelets recover to at least baseline levels for prior cycle, OR ANC ≥1x10^9/L and platelets ≥50x10^9/L, OR disease is stable/improved and cytopenias considered disease-related.

dose_modifications.condition: Dosing interruption within a cycle <7 days
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt the next dose (maintain minimum 6 days between doses).

dose_modifications.condition: Dosing interruption ≥7 days within a cycle
dose_modifications.notes: Omit the next dose within the cycle.

dose_modifications.condition: Dosing interruption ≥14 days
dose_modifications.notes: Once adequate recovery achieved, decrease total dose by 25% for subsequent cycle. If further reduction needed, reduce to 2 doses per cycle. If 25% decrease plus 2 doses per cycle not tolerated, permanently discontinue.

dose_modifications.condition: Dosing interruption >28 days
dose_modifications.notes: Consider permanent discontinuation.

dose_modifications.condition: VOD/SOS or other severe liver toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue treatment.

dose_modifications.condition: Total bilirubin >1.5x ULN and AST/ALT >2.5x ULN
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt dosing until recovery to total bilirubin ≤1.5x ULN and AST/ALT ≤2.5x ULN prior to each dose. Permanently discontinue if recovery targets not achieved (unless due to Gilbert's disease or haemolysis).

dose_modifications.condition: Grade ≥2 non-haematological toxicity (Inotuzumab Ozogamicin-related)
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt treatment until recovery to Grade 1 or pre-treatment grade levels prior to each dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Infusion related reaction
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt infusion and manage medically. Consider discontinuation depending on severity. For severe or life-threatening reactions permanently discontinue.

pre_medications: Hydrocortisone sodium succinate 100mg intravenous (30 minutes prior to inotuzumab)
Chlorphenamine 10mg intravenous injection (30 minutes prior to inotuzumab)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (30 minutes prior to inotuzumab)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or intravenous
Rasburicase 7.5mg intravenous infusion over 30 minutes in 50mL sodium chloride 0.9% (cycle 1 day 1 for TLS prevention)
Aciclovir 400mg twice a day oral (anti-infective prophylaxis)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg once a day Monday, Wednesday and Friday oral
Allopurinol 300mg once a day oral for 7 days (TLS prophylaxis, if rasburicase not given)
special_warnings: CRITICAL: Veno-occlusive disease/sinusoidal obstruction syndrome (VOD/SOS) is a serious and potentially life-threatening risk, especially with increasing number of cycles. Monitor closely for hyperbilirubinaemia >34 micromol/L, ascites, sudden weight gain >2.5% baseline, and painful hepatomegaly.
Contra-indicated in patients with prior confirmed severe or ongoing VOD/SOS.
Inotuzumab is contra-indicated in patients with serious ongoing hepatic disease (cirrhosis, nodular regenerative hyperplasia, active hepatitis).
For patients proceeding to HSCT, recommended duration is 2 cycles. Maximum 6 cycles for patients not proceeding to HSCT. Discontinue if CR/CRi not achieved within 3 cycles.
Patients should be admitted as inpatients for most of the first two weeks of cycle 1 due to risk of TLS and cytokine release.
Inotuzumab is light sensitive; protect IV bag from light using amber or dark brown cover during infusion.
QT prolongation risk; cardiac monitoring may be required.
Weigh patient daily and maintain strict fluid balance chart. Daily FBC, U&Es and LFTs required.
Tumour lysis syndrome risk; prophylaxis and monitoring essential.
Dose banding in accordance with national dose bands.

drugs.notes: 400mg twice daily (approximately 12 hours apart), days 1-28 inclusive. Must NOT be taken with food; no food 2 hours before or 1 hour after each dose. Swallow hard capsules whole with water. Available as 150mg and 200mg hard capsules. 12 cycles will be set in ARIA.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils <0.5 x10^9/L (accelerated phase - imatinib resistance/intolerance)
dose_modifications.notes: Stop nilotinib and monitor blood counts. If neutrophils recover to ≥1x10^9/L within 2 weeks, resume at 400mg twice a day. If neutrophils remain <1x10^9/L, resume at reduced dose of 400mg once a day.

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets <10 x10^9/L (accelerated phase - imatinib resistance/intolerance)
dose_modifications.notes: Stop nilotinib and monitor blood counts. If platelets recover to ≥20x10^9/L within 2 weeks, resume at 400mg twice a day. If platelets remain <20x10^9/L, resume at reduced dose of 400mg once a day.

dose_modifications.condition: Bilirubin >3x ULN and/or AST/ALT >5x ULN - hepatic impairment
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose to 400mg once a day or interrupt therapy.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3-4 serum lipase or amylase elevations
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce nilotinib dose to 400mg once a day or interrupt treatment.

special_warnings: QTc prolongation risk: baseline ECG required for all patients. Patients with prolonged QTc or high risk should be treated with extreme caution and may require regular ECG surveillance.
CRITICAL food interaction: nilotinib must NOT be taken with food. No food 2 hours before dose and no food for at least 1 hour after dose.
Cardiovascular risk assessment required at diagnosis; consider alternative TKI for those with high cardiovascular risk.
Nilotinib is a CYP3A4 substrate and inhibitor; avoid concomitant CYP3A4 inducers or potent inhibitors.
Avoid proton pump inhibitors where possible as raised pH reduces absorption of nilotinib.
Check Hepatitis B, C and HIV status prior to starting therapy.
Correct magnesium, potassium and phosphate abnormalities before starting nilotinib.
Blood lipid profile monitoring required at baseline, 3 months, 6 months and yearly.
TSH monitoring at baseline then yearly or as clinically indicated.
National Patient Safety Alert (NPSA/2008/RRR001) applies to nilotinib as oral chemotherapy.

drugs.notes: 300mg twice daily (approximately 12 hours apart), days 1-28 inclusive. Must NOT be taken with food; no food 2 hours before or 1 hour after each dose. Swallow hard capsules whole with water. For patients intolerant of or resistant to prior therapies the dose is 400mg twice a day. Available as 150mg and 200mg hard capsules. 12 cycles will be set in ARIA.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils <1 x10^9/L (chronic phase)
dose_modifications.notes: Stop nilotinib and monitor blood counts. If neutrophils recover to ≥1x10^9/L within 2 weeks, resume at 300mg twice a day. If neutrophils remain <1x10^9/L, resume at reduced dose of 400mg once a day.

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets <50 x10^9/L (chronic phase)
dose_modifications.notes: Stop nilotinib and monitor blood counts. If platelets recover to ≥50x10^9/L within 2 weeks, resume at 300mg twice a day. If platelets remain <50x10^9/L, resume at reduced dose of 400mg once a day.

dose_modifications.condition: Bilirubin >3x ULN and/or AST/ALT >5x ULN - hepatic impairment
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose to 400mg once a day or interrupt therapy.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3-4 serum lipase or amylase elevations
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce nilotinib dose to 400mg once a day or interrupt treatment.

special_warnings: QTc prolongation risk: baseline ECG required for all patients. Patients with prolonged QTc or high risk should be treated with extreme caution and may require regular ECG surveillance.
CRITICAL food interaction: nilotinib must NOT be taken with food. No food 2 hours before dose and no food for at least 1 hour after dose.
Cardiovascular risk assessment required at diagnosis; consider alternative TKI for those with high cardiovascular risk.
Nilotinib is a CYP3A4 substrate and inhibitor; avoid concomitant CYP3A4 inducers or potent inhibitors.
Avoid proton pump inhibitors where possible as raised pH reduces absorption of nilotinib.
Check Hepatitis B, C and HIV status prior to starting therapy.
Correct magnesium, potassium and phosphate abnormalities before starting nilotinib.
Blood lipid profile monitoring required at baseline, 3 months, 6 months and yearly.
TSH monitoring at baseline then yearly or as clinically indicated.
National Patient Safety Alert (NPSA/2008/RRR001) applies to nilotinib as oral chemotherapy.

drugs.notes: Cycle 1 Day 1 only: 100mg in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% at 25mg/hour (over 240 minutes). Do not increase the rate. If completed without interruption, 900mg second bag may be given same day or next day (Day 2).
Cycle 1 Day 2 (or Day 1 continued): 900mg in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% starting at 50mg/hour, escalating in increments of 50mg/hour every 30 minutes to maximum 400mg/hour.

Cycle 1 Days 8 and 15: 1000mg in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9%, start at 100mg/hour, increase by 100mg/hour every 30 minutes to max 400mg/hour. Pre-medication required before each infusion. | CYCLE 1 DAYS 8 AND 15 (1000mg): multiple Obinutuzumab entries exist — 100mg day 1 (split-dose initiation), 900mg day 2 (remainder of cycle 1 loading), 1000mg days 8 and 15 (cycle 1 continuation), and 1000mg day 1 cycles 2-6 (maintenance). All entries are intentional.

Cycles 2-6 Day 1: 1000mg in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% at 100mg/hour increasing to max 400mg/hour. Cycles 3-6 Day 1 only (no obinutuzumab cycles 7-12). Pre-medication required before each infusion. | CYCLES 2-6 DAY 1 (1000mg): multiple Obinutuzumab entries exist — 100mg day 1 (split-dose initiation), 900mg day 2 (remainder of cycle 1 loading), 1000mg days 8 and 15 (cycle 1 continuation), and 1000mg day 1 cycles 2-6 (maintenance). All entries are intentional.

Cycle 1 Days 22-28: 20mg once daily (ramp-up week 1). Take with food and a full glass of water. Venetoclax ramp-up schedule: Cycle 1 days 22-28 = 20mg; Cycle 2 days 1-7 = 50mg, days 8-14 = 100mg, days 15-21 = 200mg, days 22-28 = 400mg; Cycles 3-6 days 1-28 = 400mg; Cycles 7-12 days 1-28 = 400mg (maintenance, no obinutuzumab). Available as 10mg, 50mg, 100mg tablets. | RAMP-UP SCHEDULE: multiple Venetoclax entries exist for this protocol, each representing a different weekly dose in the ramp-up schedule (20mg → 50mg → 100mg → 200mg → 400mg). All entries are intentional.

Cycle 2 Days 1-7: 50mg once daily (ramp-up week 2). Take with food. During ramp-up, take venetoclax in the morning to facilitate laboratory monitoring. | RAMP-UP SCHEDULE: multiple Venetoclax entries exist for this protocol, each representing a different weekly dose in the ramp-up schedule (20mg → 50mg → 100mg → 200mg → 400mg). All entries are intentional.

Cycle 2 Days 8-14: 100mg once daily (ramp-up week 3). Take with food. | RAMP-UP SCHEDULE: multiple Venetoclax entries exist for this protocol, each representing a different weekly dose in the ramp-up schedule (20mg → 50mg → 100mg → 200mg → 400mg). All entries are intentional.

Cycle 2 Days 15-21: 200mg once daily (ramp-up week 4). Take with food. | RAMP-UP SCHEDULE: multiple Venetoclax entries exist for this protocol, each representing a different weekly dose in the ramp-up schedule (20mg → 50mg → 100mg → 200mg → 400mg). All entries are intentional.

Cycle 2 Days 22-28 (and all days Cycles 3-12): 400mg once daily. Take with food. Cycles 7-12 are venetoclax maintenance only (no obinutuzumab). Total treatment is 12 cycles (6 treatment + 6 maintenance). | RAMP-UP SCHEDULE: multiple Venetoclax entries exist for this protocol, each representing a different weekly dose in the ramp-up schedule (20mg → 50mg → 100mg → 200mg → 400mg). All entries are intentional.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3 or 4 haematological toxicity delaying treatment <4 weeks - obinutuzumab
dose_modifications.notes: Hold obinutuzumab until neutrophils ≥1x10^9/L and platelets ≥100x10^9/L then restart at usual dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3 or 4 haematological toxicity delaying treatment >4 weeks - obinutuzumab
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue obinutuzumab.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3 or 4 febrile neutropenia, grade 4 haematological toxicity (except lymphopenia) - venetoclax
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold venetoclax until toxicity resolves to NCI-CTC grade 1 or baseline level, then restart at same dose. If toxicity recurs, follow dose reduction table (reduce by one dose level). Discontinue if dose reduction to <100mg required for >2 weeks.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 2 or 3 related non-haematological toxicity - obinutuzumab
dose_modifications.notes: Hold obinutuzumab until toxicity resolves to grade 1 or less.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 2 non-haematological toxicity delaying treatment >4 weeks - obinutuzumab
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue obinutuzumab.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 4 non-haematological toxicity, severe haemorrhage, severe skin reaction, pneumonitis, severe arrhythmias or severe cardiovascular events - obinutuzumab
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue obinutuzumab.

dose_modifications.condition: Viral hepatitis or serious infections; hepatitis B reactivation
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue obinutuzumab.

dose_modifications.condition: TLS - hyperkalaemia (potassium >ULN)
dose_modifications.notes: Hold venetoclax until resolved. If potassium ≥6.0mmol/L or symptomatic, seek specialist renal advice.

dose_modifications.condition: TLS - hyperuricaemia (uric acid >ULN)
dose_modifications.notes: Hold venetoclax until resolved. Consider rasburicase if not given in last 24 hours.

dose_modifications.condition: TLS - hypocalcaemia (adjusted calcium <LLN or symptomatic)
dose_modifications.notes: Hold venetoclax until resolved. Administer calcium gluconate 10% 10-20ml in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 15 minutes with ECG monitoring.

dose_modifications.condition: TLS - hyperphosphataemia (phosphate >ULN)
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold venetoclax until phosphate <1.45mmol/L. Discuss with renal team regarding phosphate binders.

dose_modifications.condition: TLS - creatinine increase ≥25% from baseline or above ULN
dose_modifications.notes: Hold venetoclax until resolved. Administer intravenous fluids.

pre_medications: Methylprednisolone sodium succinate 80mg intravenous (60 minutes prior to obinutuzumab - Cycle 1 Days 1 and 2, and for patients with grade 3 IRR history or lymphocyte >25x10^9/L)
Chlorphenamine 10mg intravenous (60 minutes prior to obinutuzumab - Cycle 1 Days 1 and 2, and for patients with grade 1-2 or grade 3 IRR history)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (60 minutes prior to obinutuzumab - all cycles all days)
Sodium chloride 0.9% 500ml intravenous infusion over 60 minutes (prior to obinutuzumab)
Allopurinol 300mg oral starting 72 hours before first venetoclax dose for 7 days total (TLS prophylaxis; not included in ARIA)
Aciclovir 400mg twice a day oral (anti-infective prophylaxis)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg once a day Monday, Wednesday and Friday oral
Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day when required oral (antiemetic, cycles 1 and 2 only)
special_warnings: CRITICAL: Tumour lysis syndrome (TLS) risk with both obinutuzumab and venetoclax ramp-up. Low-risk protocol but TLS assessment mandatory before starting. Monitor electrolytes (potassium, uric acid, phosphate, calcium, creatinine, LDH) at 6-8 hours and 24 hours after first venetoclax dose and each dose increase.

Venetoclax ramp-up schedule must be followed strictly: 20mg (week 1) → 50mg (week 2) → 100mg (week 3) → 200mg (week 4) → 400mg (week 5 onwards). Do not re-escalate without TLS risk reassessment if interrupted >1 week during titration or >2 weeks at 400mg.

Obinutuzumab infusion related reactions: most common during first cycle. Appropriately pre-medicate before each infusion. Withhold antihypertensive treatments for 12 hours before, during and 1 hour after obinutuzumab infusion.
Progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML) risk with obinutuzumab; monitor for new or worsening neurological symptoms.
Grapefruit products, Seville oranges and starfruit must be avoided during venetoclax treatment (CYP3A4 interaction).
Venetoclax interacts with many other medications; always check drug interactions.
Hepatitis B, C and HIV status must be checked prior to starting treatment. Hepatitis B positive patients require specialist input.
Tumour burden assessment (CT scan) required prior to starting therapy.
12 cycles total: 6 treatment cycles (obinutuzumab + venetoclax) and 6 maintenance cycles (venetoclax only). Total treatment duration approximately 12 months.
National Patient Safety Alert (NPSA/2008/RRR001) applies to venetoclax as oral chemotherapy.

drugs.notes: 4mg/m² intravenous bolus on days 1 and 14 of each 28-day cycle. Pentostatin administered every 14 days until maximum response (normalisation of blood counts), then 2 further doses given. On average 10 doses required. If no partial response (based on blood counts) after 4 doses, discontinue. 6 cycles will be set in ARIA. Dose banded according to national dose bands (2mg/ml).

dose_modifications.condition: Creatinine clearance >59 ml/min
dose_modifications.notes: Full dose 4mg/m².

dose_modifications.condition: Creatinine clearance 40-59 ml/min
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose to 3mg/m². Discuss with consultant; alternatives such as rituximab or interferon may be considered until renal function normalises.

dose_modifications.condition: Creatinine clearance 35-39 ml/min
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose to 2mg/m². Discuss with consultant before prescribing.

dose_modifications.condition: Creatinine clearance <35 ml/min
dose_modifications.notes: Not recommended. Discuss with consultant; alternatives may be considered.

dose_modifications.condition: Absolute neutrophil count <0.2 x10^9/L (in patient whose initial neutrophil count was >0.5 x10^9/L)
dose_modifications.notes: Temporarily withhold pentostatin until pre-dose levels are attained. No pre-treatment dose reductions for anaemia, neutropenia or thrombocytopenia.

pre_medications: Sodium chloride 0.9% 500ml intravenous infusion over 60 minutes (pre-hydration before pentostatin)
Sodium chloride 0.9% 500ml intravenous infusion over 60 minutes (post-hydration after pentostatin)
Allopurinol 300mg once a day oral for 7 days (cycle 1 only)
Aciclovir 400mg twice a day oral (anti-infective prophylaxis starting day 6, continued until lymphocytes >1x10^9/L)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg once a day Monday, Wednesday and Friday oral (anti-infective prophylaxis)
special_warnings: CRITICAL: Patients treated with pentostatin carry a LIFELONG risk of transfusion-associated graft versus host disease (TA-GVHD). ALL blood products required for these patients must be IRRADIATED FOR LIFE. Local blood transfusion departments must be notified immediately when decision to treat is made. Patient must be issued with an alert card to carry at all times.
No antiemetics are required with pentostatin.
Pre and post hydration with sodium chloride 0.9% 500ml over 60 minutes is required at each administration.
FBC, LFTs and U&Es required prior to initiating treatment and before day 1 of each cycle.
Renal function must be monitored; dose adjustments required for renal impairment (see dose modifications). Consult before prescribing in renal impairment.
Hepatic impairment: no information available; use with caution.
Anti-infective prophylaxis (aciclovir and co-trimoxazole) starting day 6 and continued until lymphocytes >1x10^9/L.
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.
Treatment continues to complete response plus 2 additional doses, or stop after partial response at 12 months.
